name: Digital Product Passport (DPP)
thesis: Is the EU Digital Product Passport becoming real on schedule - on the regulator's side, and on industry's side?

scores.regulator_delivery_note: Of everything the EU committed to deliver by now, how much landed - and on time. Computed over milestones that are due only; both items past their date so far (the service-provider act and the DPP registry set-up) slipped, so the score sits at 0.

this_week_read.headline: Second call graded, second miss: the registry's set-up deadline landed without the rules to accept registrations.
this_week_read.body: The EU Central DPP Registry's 19 July 2026 legal deadline (ESPR Art. 13) has passed. The Commission has not announced a formal delay to the set-up date itself, but the implementing regulation that actually governs registration - the process, required data fields and verification mechanics - remains unadopted; its public consultation closed 27 May 2026 and a final version is not expected before autumn 2026. Independent trackers report the production registration interface is not yet public. yellow3 leaned at_risk and calls this a miss: a registry that cannot yet process a registration is not operational and accepting registrations in the sense the call asked about. That is the instrument's second resolved call, and its second miss - regulator delivery stays at 0. Elsewhere, CEN-CENELEC's last two DPP standards (prEN 18239, prEN 18246) closed their formal vote on 16 July 2026 but are not yet published as EN; six of eight remain live, none yet cited in the Official Journal.

milestones.label: DPP Registry operational and accepting registrations
milestones.note: 19 Jul 2026 set-up deadline reached, but the implementing regulation governing the registration process, data fields and verification mechanics is still unadopted (consultation closed 27 May 2026, adoption not expected until autumn 2026); the production registration interface is not public. Treated as slipped pending a confirmed operational launch.

milestones.label: DPP system standards published (EN)
milestones.note: EN 18216, 18219-18223 published 25 June 2026; the last two, prEN 18239 and prEN 18246 (security/confidentiality and data authentication), were under formal vote until 16 July 2026 and are not yet published as EN.

milestones.label: Standards harmonised (cited in the Official Journal)
milestones.note: Publication is not harmonisation. OJ citation, which creates the presumption of conformity, is still pending.

milestones.label: Customs interconnection live
milestones.note: Four years after the registry rules enter into force.

milestones.label: DPP service-provider delegated act adopted
milestones.note: Still a draft delegated act (requirements for DPP service providers plus a conformity-assessment scheme); not adopted.

milestones.label: Iron and steel ESPR delegated act adopted
milestones.note: First priority product group; not yet adopted, timelines drifting.

milestones.label: Textiles, tyres, aluminium ESPR acts adopted
Mattresses and ICT ESPR acts adopted
Energy-related product rules updated
milestones.note: Rolling range, tracked as ongoing progress.

signals.name: Active DPP pilots across value chains
Public DPP solutions / vendors live
signals.source: Market mapping / CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice
signals.note: ~15 established public DPP solution vendors (Circularise, Kezzler, Scantrust, Spherity, OPTEL, Protokol and peers); the CIRPASS-2 Community of Practice now lists 111 participants.

signals.name: GS1 Digital Link adoption proxy
signals.note: GS1 Sunrise 2027 - the 2D / Digital Link migration most DPP carriers ride on - is in pilot across 48 countries representing 88% of world GDP.

signals.name: Implementable standards (published as EN)
signals.note: Of the 8 JTC 24 areas, how many are stable enough to build against today.

calls.statement: The EU DPP Registry will be operational and accepting registrations by its legal deadline of 19 July 2026.
calls.grading_note: The 19 July 2026 Article 13 set-up deadline passed without the registry genuinely accepting registrations: the implementing regulation defining the registration process, required data fields and verification mechanics is still unadopted (consultation closed 27 May 2026, adoption expected autumn 2026), and independent trackers confirm the production registration interface is not yet public.

calls.statement: The DPP service-provider delegated act will be adopted by 30 June 2026.
calls.grading_note: The 30 June 2026 deadline passed with the delegated act on DPP service providers still a draft, not adopted. The related DPP-registry implementing regulation only closed public consultation on 27 May 2026 (Ares(2026)4424976), and the Commission's DPP rules remain in technical preparation.

calls.statement: All eight JTC 24 DPP system standards will be published as European Standards by 30 September 2026.
At least one DPP standard will be cited in the EU Official Journal (presumption of conformity) by 31 December 2026.
The iron and steel ESPR delegated act will be adopted within 2026.
The textiles ESPR delegated act will not be adopted before 2027.

methodology: Track A scores the EU's own delivery against the dates the regulation set, over milestones that are due only, weighted (registry and the service-provider act count most). delivered_on_time = 1.0, delivered_late = 0.5, slipped/missed = 0, upcoming is excluded until its date passes. Track B is a provisional proxy of industry readiness and is tagged as such until the proprietary onboarding signal replaces the basket. Calls are falsifiable, dated, and graded HIT or MISS in public, with a running win-rate.

source.line: Sources: Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 (ESPR); European Commission DPP FAQ v1.0 (DG GROW); European Commission DPP Registry page; CEN-CENELEC JTC 24; EUR-Lex. Verified 19 July 2026.
